_meta.schema_enforcement: inline JSON Schema — output validated against code-reviewer.yaml output_schema before filing

findings.description: Threat API URL read from config without enforcing HTTPS. Misconfigured THREAT_API_URL could cause requests to internal infrastructure.
findings.remediation: Joi .uri() in config.js already enforces valid URI. Added production guard: if THREAT_API_URL is empty in production, throw 500 instead of silently skipping the check.
findings.fix_applied: Added production guard in threatCheck.js and Joi URI validation in config.js

findings.description: new URL() accepts javascript:/data: URIs in some environments if protocol check runs after parsing.
findings.remediation: Pre-check protocol with regex before parsing.
findings.fix_applied: Added /^https?:\/\//i pre-check before new URL() call

findings.description: Empty THREAT_API_URL silently skips threat check — accidental production deploy without config bypasses security.
findings.vulnerable_code: if (!config.threatApiUrl) { return { isMalicious: false, reason: null }; }
findings.remediation: Throw 500 in production if THREAT_API_URL is unset.
findings.fix_applied: Added config.env === 'production' guard that throws INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR

findings.description: ApiError constructor used incorrectly — existingShortCode data silently dropped, client cannot identify the existing shortened URL.
findings.vulnerable_code: throw new ApiError(httpStatus.CONFLICT, 'URL already shortened', true, '', { existingShortCode: ... });
findings.remediation: Attach data to error object after construction: err.data = { existingShortCode }; Update error handler middleware to include err.data in response.
findings.fix_applied: err.data pattern in link.service.js + error.js middleware updated to spread err.data

findings.description: User search input built directly into RegExp without escaping — ReDoS risk.
findings.fix_applied: Added regex escape before RegExp construction

findings.description: Full URL logged on threat check failure — URL query params may contain credentials/tokens.
findings.fix_applied: Changed to log domain only with try/catch for parse safety

findings.description: Double-swallowing of analytics errors (in service AND controller) makes debugging analytics outages impossible.
findings.vulnerable_code: } catch (err) { logger.warn(...); }  // in service, AND .catch(() => {}) in controller
findings.remediation: Remove try/catch from recordClick — single swallow point in redirect controller.
findings.fix_applied: Removed try/catch from click.service.js; redirect.controller.js .catch(() => {}) is sole swallow

metadata.instruction_3_3_note: This file documents the schema-enforced output required by instruction 3.3. The JSON structure above was validated against the inline JSON Schema defined in _meta.schema before being saved.
